details: The Chief Minister Rural Drinking Water Nishchay Yojana was launched in 2016 to ensure every rural household in Bihar has access to safe drinking water through a piped supply system. The scheme is implemented in phases by Gram Panchayats, covering 5,013 Panchayats where water quality is not compromised or is not already being handled by the Public Health Engineering Department. The water supply aims for a maximum of 70 liters per person per day, with strong community participation in operation and maintenance.
benefits: Improved Access to Clean Drinking Water:
Provides safe and clean drinking water to rural households, ensuring better health and hygiene.
Ensures a reliable and uninterrupted supply of water to rural areas throughout the year.
Helps in reducing the incidence of waterborne diseases by providing purified drinking water.
eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of a rural area in Bihar.
The applicant must not already have a piped water supply connection.
The applicant should belong to a household requiring access to safe drinking water.
The applicant must comply with the guidelines set by the Gram Panchayat.
The applicant should be willing to contribute to the operation and maintenance of the water supply system.

offline: Fill in the application form with the required personal and household details.
Submit supporting documents such as identity proof and address proof.
The application will be reviewed for eligibility.
Upon approval, the household will be included in the piped water supply scheme.

documents_required: Identity proof (Aadhaar card, Voter ID, etc.).
Residential proof of the Gram Panchayat.

faqs.question: What is the "Mukhyamantri Gramin Peyjal Nishchay Yojana"?
faqs.answer: This scheme aimed at providing safe drinking water through pipelines to rural households in Bihar.

faqs.question: When was this scheme launched?
faqs.answer: The scheme was launched in 2016 and is expected to be completed by 2020.

faqs.question: How much water will be supplied to each household?
faqs.answer: Each household will receive a maximum of 70 liters of drinking water per person per day.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Residents of rural Bihar who do not already have access to piped drinking water.

faqs.question: What documents are required to apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants must provide identity proof, address proof, and details about their water requirements.

faqs.question: How can someone apply for the "Mukhyamantri Gramin Peyjal Nishchay Yojana"?
faqs.answer: Applications can be submitted through the Gram Panchayat office, where applicants must fill out a form and submit the required documents.

faqs.question: Is there any fee associated with applying for the scheme?
faqs.answer: No, there is no fee for applying to the Mukhyamantri Gramin Peyjal Nishchay Yojana.

faqs.question: Will the water supply be continuous throughout the year?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme ensures a continuous supply of clean drinking water to rural households throughout the year.

faqs.question: What role do Gram Panchayats play in this scheme?
faqs.answer: Gram Panchayats are responsible for implementing, monitoring, and managing the water supply in rural areas, ensuring the operation and maintenance of the systems.

faqs.question: How will disputes related to the implementation of the scheme be handled?
faqs.answer: Any disputes arising during the implementation of the Mukhyamantri Gramin Peyjal Nishchay Yojana will be resolved by the District Water and Sanitation Committee, which holds the final authority in such matters.
